name: Bosnia and Herzegovina

colors.symbolism: neutrality, peace and association with Europe (EU/UN/Council of Europe blue)
the sun, hope and the natural beauty of the country; the triangle's three points represent the country's three constituent peoples (Bosniaks, Croats and Serbs)
peace; the seven full and two half stars suggest continuity with Europe

description: A blue field with a yellow right-angled isosceles triangle along the top edge, its hypotenuse running diagonally from upper-fly to lower-hoist; a row of seven full and two half white five-pointed stars runs along this diagonal, the top and bottom stars being cut off by the edges of the flag.
history: Adopted on 4 February 1998 to replace the previous flag (a white field with the medieval blue shield of the Kotromanić dynasty and the fleurs-de-lis), which had become identified with the Bosniak side during the 1992–1995 war. The neutral design was imposed by High Representative Carlos Westendorp after the country's parliament could not agree on a flag, with the deliberate use of pan-European blue and gold (echoing the EU flag) chosen to be acceptable to all three constituent peoples.

features.symbolism: neutrality and peace; deliberately echoes the blue of the European Union and the United Nations
the three points represent the three constituent peoples — Bosniaks, Croats and Serbs — and the triangle as a whole evokes the rough triangular shape of the country on the map
Europe and the European character of Bosnia and Herzegovina; the row is shown extending beyond the field, the two end stars being cut off, to suggest infinite continuity
